Customers pay with the EatClub card anywhere → they earn instant cashback (funded by EatClub) → they return to redeem it at participating venues like yours.What it means for you
You accept the EatClub card at all times; even without running deals. [{author":"Gabriella Szabo";"datetime":"Wed; 01 Apr 2026 02:35:18 GMT";"note":"1.	We’ve noticed some customers are using loopholes in the deal. For example; they book after the deal time; order within the hour to qualify; pay within that window; but then continue sitting for much longer (e.g. deal at 5:30; booking at 6:00; order at 6:20; pay at 6:30; but stay until 8:00). They are using all the loopholes in the system.
	2.	We’re also seeing more groups with lower spend (e.g. kids or minimal orders); which affects the overall image of the restaurant. When new customers walk past and see this; it doesn’t reflect the dining experience we aim to present.
	3.	The “pay within 1 hour” rule doesn’t seem effective in practice. Customers are still staying well beyond that time; which defeats the purpose of having the rule in place. Also; the additional 15-minute allowance for late arrivals makes it even harder to manage.

Overall; as I know; we seem to be the only venue asking the 1-hour payment window. Considering this; it may be best for us to step away; as it doesn’t quite suit our operations.
";"id":"x2azN4dQT5i1XbaATxVzo"}]"

Two weeks ago; your Australian regional manager (a female manager) and another person who was introduced as the future account manager for our restaurant visited our premises unannounced to resolve this dispute. To our shock; this female manager was highly unprofessional; rude; aggressive; and coercive — pressuring us to offer discounts in a manner that was verbally offensive and deeply distressing.

During that meeting; I clearly stated that we wanted to quit the platform and cease all cooperation immediately. She promised to terminate our partnership that same evening.

However; I have just received a new email indicating that SUSHI MAMI is still active on your platform. Because of your platform’s policy — where customers are not identified at the door — we have continued to suffer losses due to this ongoing arrangement.

I did not wish to file a complaint about your platform’s poor service; but this level of unprofessionalism and irresponsibility is completely unacceptable.

Please immediately terminate all cooperation with SUSHI MAMI. Confirm the termination in writing by email or by phone during business hours. If this is done promptly; our company will not pursue the matter further.

Thank you.

Sincerely;



Renee

[{author":"Ryan Bhambra";"datetime":"Wed; 08 Apr 2026 16:34:49 GMT";"note":"I’ve decided it’s time for us to take a pause with Eat Club. I’d like to stop all future promotions from the end of this week for both LARDO and bastardo.

I wanted to be clear with you both on my reasons why. I’ve been pretty uncertain for a while but with some analysis of the last few weeks I can say for sure that the platform is not working as intended for us.

The reason I asked to cut the offers back a few weeks ago was to understand more clearly how it was being used from a smaller set of client data. 

I can only speak to my own experience but it’s my belief that EatClub is failing to achieve the core goal set out by the company to ‘fill empty space’ and is actually driving a discounter economy which is to the detriment of both restaurants and customers. 

This marketplace mindset encourages inflated pricing to justify discounts and trains customers to seek out deals at any opportunity.

From the 4 weeks over 45% of the redemptions made were on tables with existing bookings via our system - That’s people who would have dined with us anyway.

I think some of this is directly linked serious functionality challenges for a business like LARDO. 

- claimed offer at 1830; arrived at 1930 for an existing booking; paid at 2130 - no impact or limits or ability to manage or police. 
- existing offers cannot be isolated (a lunch deal ends up costing us money to provide)
- people claiming discounts halfway through an existing booking - one example
- a party of 8 with a set menu realised one of them had EatClub and claimed a discount part-way thought.
- many customers turn up; and it’s not set up; they don’t know how to use it. and they were coming anyway but saw they could get it cheaper; is alarming. 

I also think the universal payment feature you’re adding to the platform is a bad move for your partners. 8% off any potential bill; at any time isn’t a logical way to generate footfall or drive brand value for my business. 

Can you please confirm you’ll remove us from the platform by the end of the week? 

Thanks
Nathan 


Mikey; Myself and Sam McKenzie have reviewed and I have asked for a sit down meeting with Nathan. Big part of this was integrations where we cant as he with Toast ";"id":"djlXIxSlwRGYJQY4MDv5m"}]"
